metadata.title: The CRQC / quantum-advantage landscape - claims, the Q-Day clock, national programs, and the counter-migration
metadata.provenance: Web-verified (Trail of Bits blog; arXiv/Nature on random-circuit sampling; NSA CNSA 2.0; NIST; Qureca/CSIS/MERICS/USCC; G7/EU/UK/China program figures). Claims graded fact|contested.
metadata.ties: Pairs with macro-quantum-computing.json (the equities/threat side) and macro-pqc-chips.json (the PQC/defense side). Core repo ethos rhymes: CLAIMS NEED REAL VERIFICATION - and Google's quantum-cryptanalysis 'proof' was itself broken (below).

supremacy_claims_and_skepticism.grade: claims CONTESTED; the proof-fragility finding STRONG
supremacy_claims_and_skepticism.trail_of_bits: Apr 17 2026: Trail of Bits 'We beat Google's zero-knowledge proof of quantum cryptanalysis' - exploited memory-safety + logic vulnerabilities in Google's Rust zkVM to FORGE a proof claiming better quantum-circuit performance than Google's own results. The verifiability infrastructure behind the claim was unsound. (Distinct from, but compounds, the broader supremacy skepticism.)
supremacy_claims_and_skepticism.broader_skepticism: Google Sycamore (2019) + Willow (2024) random-circuit-sampling 'supremacy/advantage' claims have been repeatedly narrowed: classical tensor-network algorithms have spoofed/matched results; IBM disputed the 2019 claim; verification of the sampled distribution in the supremacy regime is missing; classical advantage returns beyond ~a few hundred qubits at near-term fidelities.

crqc_timeline.grade: STRONG that the threat is real + compressing; 'Q-Day date' CONTESTED
crqc_timeline.fact: A cryptographically-relevant quantum computer (CRQC) that breaks RSA-2048 is commonly projected ~2030 ±3 yrs. Resource estimates are COMPRESSING: 2025-26 papers cut the qubits needed to break RSA-2048 from ~20M to <1M, potentially ~100k with newer architectures (Gidney/Google et al.) - the timeline is moving toward us, not away.

harvest_now_decrypt_later.fact: NSA (Aug 2021): 'Adversaries may be collecting encrypted data now, waiting for the day when quantum computers can decrypt it.' Analysis of 100+ sources: ~95-100% of government-classified and ~98-100% of healthcare data encrypted TODAY is exposed to retroactive decryption. The damage to long-lived secrets is ALREADY being locked in - this is the key point: the loss is happening now, the decryption is deferred.
harvest_now_decrypt_later.tnfl_the_integrity_twin: HNDL is the CONFIDENTIALITY threat (collect ciphertext now, decrypt later). Its INTEGRITY twin is 'Trust Now, Forge Later' (TNFL): once a CRQC (Shor) derives private keys from public keys, previously-issued DIGITAL SIGNATURES become FORGEABLE - so old certificates, signed identity assertions, and ePassport Passive-Authentication signatures can be retroactively forged/impersonated. HNDL breaks secrecy; TNFL breaks AUTHENTICATION - and authentication is exactly what a digital ID is for. This is the mechanism behind the identity-proof paradox in spec-uk-labour-tbi-influence (#68): the credential's whole value inverts into impersonability at Q-Day. grade: fact (Shor breaks RSA/ECC signatures) + labeled (the 'forge later' framing).

digital_id_pqc_migration_status.summary: How far the DIGITAL-ID world actually is from quantum-safe, as of mid-2026: essentially NO major deployed national/consumer digital-ID system is post-quantum; the vast majority still run RSA/ECC (breakable by Shor). A handful of pilots/enablers are ahead. This is the empirical backing for the identity-proof paradox (spec-uk-labour-tbi-influence #68) and the 'governments change slower than the threat' point. Web-verified 2026-06 (prompted by a shared Grok exchange whose checkable claims verify).
still_classical_the_majority: EU EUDI Wallet (eIDAS 2.0): current prototypes/implementations use RSA/ECC; PQC is only in discussion/recommendation; ENISA EU-wide certification only after 2026 - NOT live with PQC (offered to all citizens 2026, businesses must accept from 2027 - i.e. mass rollout on classical crypto).

ICAO eMRTDs (ePassports): Doc 9303 Passive Authentication relies on RSA/ECDSA Document-Signer signatures; ICAO is updating Doc 9303 for quantum-safe algorithms but most deployed documents remain classical.
National eIDs (Estonia, India Aadhaar, etc.) and most blockchain DIDs/SSI: generally still on quantum-vulnerable crypto, with hybrid-PQC research ongoing.

US REAL ID (driver's-license standard, enforced May 2025): NOT post-quantum - and barely a cryptographic credential at all. The card's machine-readable part is an AAMVA PDF417 BARCODE storing data in PLAINTEXT, UNSIGNED by default (an optional digital-signature subfile is allowed and used by some states/vendors, but it's classical ECDSA where present, not standard/universal). Security = physical features + DMV database lookup; an unsigned barcode also has NO cryptographic authenticity even today (clonable). The state-to-state backend (AAMVA S2S/SPEXS) runs classical TLS/PKI. The DIGITAL version - the mobile driver's license (mDL, ISO/IEC 18013-5), the Apple/Google Wallet ID - is issuer-signed via the Mobile Security Object using ECDSA = classical, quantum-fragile, NOT PQC. So REAL ID is actually BEHIND an ePassport on card-level crypto. CRYPTO-AGILITY: essentially NONE in the physical card - there's no card-level algorithm to rotate, and changing the optional signature would require an AAMVA DL/ID standard revision + 50-state RE-ISSUANCE + the entire scanner/verifier ecosystem updating (decade-scale, federated - the opposite of agile). The barcode IS versioned (an AAMVA version field), but that's a slow standards+reprint migration, not algorithm-agility. The DIGITAL mDL is the MORE crypto-agile path - software-updatable, the issuer can re-sign wallets with a new algorithm without reprinting - which is exactly the crypto-agility argument that fuels 'go digital' (and the updatable=controllable tension in digitalid-orchestration). Net: REAL ID as deployed is NOT crypto-agile; its only 'agility' is a glacial standards+reissuance cycle; agility (and the control tension) lives in the digital mDL.
ahead_pilots_and_enablers: GSA PIV PQC experiment (with Unifyia): tested issuing/managing federal PIV credentials with Dilithium (ML-DSA L2/3/5) alongside classical + hybrid (Ed25519) - an EXPERIMENT, not production; authentication hit OS/browser/hardware ecosystem gaps. (idmanagement.gov)

Microsoft SymCrypt: ships ML-KEM + ML-DSA in PRODUCTION across Azure/M365/Windows 11/Server 2025 (Nov-2025 update); AD Certificate Services ML-DSA certificate issuance GENERALLY AVAILABLE May 2026 - PQC reaching the PKI BACKEND many ID systems rely on. AWS KMS also supports ML-DSA.

SEALSQ QS7001 post-quantum secure element: a hardware-rooted PQC identity chip (the corpus's own node - fin-sealsq-wisekey-global / macro-pqc-chips, the WISeID/'sovereign ID' play) - real silicon, early ramp (#76 pipeline-vs-announced).
Wultra Post-Quantum Authentication (PQA): a vendor claiming NIST-PQC-integrated user-auth for banks/fintech/eIDAS 2.0 - vendor claim, grade accordingly.
digital_id_pqc_migration_status.the_so_what: The migration is barely begun for the exact systems being mandated NOW: a population is being onboarded to the EUDI Wallet and to national digital IDs on RSA/ECC while the NIST deadlines (deprecate 2030, disallow 2035) and the compressing CRQC estimate (<1M qubits) close in. Every credential issued today on classical crypto is a TNFL liability - forgeable post-Q-Day - and re-issuing at population scale after a break is the bootstrap-without-a-trusted-anchor problem in #68. The pilots that are ahead are backends (Microsoft PKI), experiments (GSA), or hardware chips (SEALSQ QS7001) - not the deployed national wallets.

card_expiry_reissuance_and_rural_equity.summary: The most CONCRETE, sympathetic version of the whole argument: physical eID cards / ePassports bake crypto (RSA/ECC keys + signatures) into the chip AT ISSUANCE with multi-year validity - you generally cannot OTA-swap a chip's signing ALGORITHM - so the only migration path is REISSUANCE (or remote re-keying). This cuts two ways, and the burden concentrates rurally. Facts verified; the equity read labeled.
card_expiry_reissuance_and_rural_equity.the_mechanism: ICAO Doc 9303 ePassport Passive Authentication = RSA/ECDSA Document-Signer signatures; CA/TA use ECC - all Shor-breakable. Smartcard upgrade cycles often exceed a DECADE, far longer than ordinary cert renewal (1-2yr), so the deployed base ages on classical crypto. The planned migration is hybrid/composite keys (classical + ML-DSA) introduced AT REISSUANCE.
card_expiry_reissuance_and_rural_equity.the_kernel_of_goodness: The normal 5-10yr card-EXPIRY cycle is itself the built-in migration vehicle - issuers can swap RSA->PQC at the renewal a citizen would do anyway. Expiry is the fix, not only the problem.
card_expiry_reissuance_and_rural_equity.estonia_roca_precedent: It has ALREADY happened once, classically: the 2017 ROCA flaw in Infineon chips let attackers recover RSA private keys from the public key. Estonia found ~750,000-760,000 national eID cards affected (issued Oct 2014-2017) and SUSPENDED their certificates on 4 Nov 2017 - then fixed it via a REMOTE certificate UPDATE (Gemalto), not a physical reprint. Proof that (a) a deployed national-ID's internal crypto can become a sudden liability forcing mass action, and (b) remote re-keying can substitute for reprinting - the exact template the cost argument below leans on.
card_expiry_reissuance_and_rural_equity.the_rural_equity_problem: Reissuance burden + residual vulnerability both concentrate on the LEAST-resourced: rural populations renew slowest (distance to biometric-capture/enrollment centres, thin connectivity, older cards, lower renewal rates), so they hold the OLDEST, most quantum-fragile credentials LONGEST; and rural verifiers (border posts, clinics, banks) are slowest to update to CHECK new PQC credentials. The migration's cost and its tail-risk land on the same disadvantaged nodes - the digital-divide twin of the municipal-infrastructure burden in city_and_infrastructure_layer.
card_expiry_reissuance_and_rural_equity.grade: ICAO PA crypto + Estonia ROCA (750-760k, suspended 2017-11-04, remote re-key) + smartcard decade cycles FACT (web-verified); the rural-equity concentration is labeled interpretation

digital_id_pqc_migration_status.grade: deployment status (EUDI/ICAO/Aadhaar classical; GSA experiment; Microsoft SymCrypt/ADCS GA; HQC) FACT (web-verified); Wultra a vendor claim; the identity-proof-paradox linkage labeled

q_day_silent_window_asymmetry.summary: The game theory the public deadlines ignore: a first-mover's dominant strategy at Q-Day is SILENCE, so the real Q-Day will likely PRECEDE the public one - you cannot time a migration to a break you are never told about. The underlying facts (HNDL already incurred, deadlines public, supremacy claims unverifiable, collection apparatus documented) are fact; the concealment game theory is a labeled strategic-logic argument (NOT a claim that any actor currently HAS a CRQC).
q_day_silent_window_asymmetry.the_defenders_assumption: NIST 2030/2035 and CNSA 2.0 2027/2030/2035 implicitly assume Q-Day is OBSERVABLE - that you will know a CRQC exists in time to finish migrating. The whole public deadline structure is a defender's clock.
q_day_silent_window_asymmetry.the_concealment_incentive: A state (or actor) that achieves CRQC first has a DOMINANT STRATEGY OF SILENCE: revealing it would (a) trigger the world's immediate PQC migration, slamming the exploitation window shut, and (b) forfeit the live intelligence windfall (decrypt the HNDL backlog + forge signatures via TNFL). So the FIRST CRQC is the one you are LEAST likely to hear about. The public 'Q-Day' lags the real one - by exactly the length of the lead the first-mover can protect.
q_day_silent_window_asymmetry.the_harvest_side_advantage: The value of being first accrues most to whoever ALSO harvested the most ciphertext/credentials. The optimal adversary play is HARVEST NOW (cheap, ongoing, low-risk) + DECRYPT/FORGE SILENTLY LATER. Best-positioned = actors with BOTH (a) collection reach (NSA/Five-Eyes upstream taps + Room 641A lineage; China's collection; the City-fibre/embassy vantage and Salt-Typhoon-style telecom breaches in spec-disclosures-surveillance / spec-telecom-satellite / spec-uk-labour-tbi-influence) AND (b) a credible CRQC path (US national labs / Google / IBM; China's program). Even a brief lead (months) is decisive for long-lived secrets and identity credentials.
q_day_silent_window_asymmetry.the_verification_double_bind: You also cannot VERIFY a rival's capability - Google's own zero-knowledge proof of quantum cryptanalysis was FORGED by Trail of Bits (supremacy_claims_and_skepticism), and supremacy results are repeatedly spoofed/contested. So neither does the attacker reveal NOR can defenders confirm: the exact signal you would use to TRIGGER an emergency migration is the signal that is suppressed, unreliable, and hype-saturated at once. Double opacity.
q_day_silent_window_asymmetry.the_conclusion: 'We have until 2030/2035' is the most dangerous assumption in the whole quantum file. The defender migrates on a PUBLIC clock; the attacker exploits on a PRIVATE one; the loss (HNDL/TNFL) is ALREADY accruing; and the trigger signal is unverifiable. The rational migration deadline is therefore 'as early as possible,' not 'by the published date' - yet the verified deployment status (digital_id_pqc_migration_status) is that essentially nothing is migrated. The gap between the private real clock and the public deferred one is the window, and it is open now.
q_day_silent_window_asymmetry.grade: HNDL-incurred + public-deadlines + forged-ZK-proof + documented-collection FACT; the concealment/first-mover game theory is labeled strategic-logic (no claim any actor has a CRQC today)

China.spend: ~$15B gov + a 1T-yuan ($138B) national VC guidance fund across AI/quantum/H2 (Mar 2025)
China.edge: LEADS quantum COMMUNICATIONS: ~12,000 km network + 2 quantum satellites (Micius)

United States.spend: National Quantum Initiative ~$1.2B (2019-24) + ~$1.8B reauth (2025-29); DOE 5 centers $625M

EU.spend: Quantum Flagship EUR 1B (2018-28); AGILE defense plan folds quantum into the European Armaments Technology Roadmap (2026)

UK.spend: National Quantum Strategy GBP 2.5B (2024-34) + GBP 2B procurement/scaling (Mar 17 2026)

national_programs.military_note: The race is increasingly MILITARY (EU: 'China and the US are already investing in military uses') - quantum sensing/PNT (GPS-denied navigation), secure comms, and codebreaking (CRQC). (Grade: STRONG.)

corporate_players.IBM: Quantum-hardware leader; public roadmap (Condor/Heron -> 'Starling' fault-tolerant target ~2029); IBM Quantum Network; also Hedera governing-council member + enterprise AI (watsonx). The 'realest' large-cap quantum program alongside Google.
corporate_players.Google: Willow chip; supremacy/advantage claims (see skepticism + Trail of Bits).
corporate_players.others: IonQ/Quantinuum (Honeywell)/PsiQuantum/Rigetti/D-Wave; Microsoft (Majorana topological); Amazon (Ocelot + Braket); China: USTC/Origin Quantum.
corporate_players.broadcom_note: Broadcom is NOT quantum - it is the custom-AI-silicon (XPU/MTIA/TPU) node already mapped in fin-google-amazon-anthropic-meta.json + fin-meta-family.json; consolidated here only to disambiguate from the quantum cohort.

counter_migration.NSA_CNSA_2_0: New National Security Systems quantum-safe by Jan 1 2027; application-layer by 2030; full infrastructure by 2035.
counter_migration.NIST: RSA-2048 / ECC P-256 deprecated 2030; ALL quantum-vulnerable public-key disallowed by 2035. Replacements: FIPS 203 (ML-KEM/Kyber), 204 (ML-DSA/Dilithium), 205 (SLH-DSA/SPHINCS+), all finalized Aug 2024; HQC (code-based) selected Mar 11 2025 as a BACKUP KEM (distinct math from ML-KEM's lattices), draft 2026 / finalize ~2027; FN-DSA (Falcon) in process.
counter_migration.EU: Coordinated PQC roadmap: national strategies + crypto inventories by end-2026; critical financial infrastructure PQC by end-2030; DORA + NIS2 + PCI-DSS 4.0 force key-management upgrades.
counter_migration.G7: G7 Cyber Expert Group (co-chaired US Treasury + Bank of England) urging banks/insurers/exchanges to accelerate PQC.
counter_migration.US_exec: Trump 2026 Cyber Strategy places PQC alongside zero-trust/AI-defense/cloud modernization for federal networks.

city_and_infrastructure_layer.grade: research-hub clusters STRONG; distinct city programs MODEST
city_and_infrastructure_layer.fact: Sub-national action is mostly (a) research/industry CLUSTERS - Chicago Quantum Exchange + Illinois Quantum & Microelectronics Park (PsiQuantum anchor, ~$500M state), Maryland 'Capital of Quantum' (UMD/IonQ), Colorado 'Elevate Quantum', Massachusetts; and (b) the migration BURDEN landing on municipal critical infrastructure (utilities, water, transit, hospitals, courts) that is least resourced to do crypto inventories + PQC migration before 2030. Cities are exposed nodes, not yet coordinated actors.

whats_really_happening: Three things at once: (1) a CLAIMS problem - quantum-advantage assertions are contested and even Google's ZK proof of quantum cryptanalysis was forged by Trail of Bits, so the 'how close is Q-Day' signal is noisy and hype-prone (mirrors the repo's whole thesis: verify claims); (2) a REAL, compressing CRQC threat with an ALREADY-INCURRED harvest-now-decrypt-later loss to today's long-lived secrets; (3) a GEOPOLITICAL race - China leads quantum comms/sensing and outspends via a $138B VC vehicle, the US/EU/UK lead compute + drive the PQC counter-migration (CNSA 2.0 / NIST / EU / G7), with deadlines 2027-2035. For this analysis: the quantum theme is BOTH a genuine national-security inflection AND a narrative-beta bubble vector (IonQ/RGTI/QBTS, SEALSQ/WISeKey) - separate the real (HNDL, programs, PQC mandates, IBM/Google/IonQ engineering) from the hype (800x-sales pure-plays, forged proofs, 'Q-Day next year' marketing).

edges.raw_instrument: led PsiQuantum $1B Series E (2025) with Nvidia NVentures/Temasek/QIA/Morgan Stanley; $7B valuation
NVentures in the $1B Series E (photonic fault-tolerant)
existing shareholder (Honeywell majority owner; Mitsui/Quanta also in)
NASA Goddard prime contract (Dirac-3) + NIST TFLN photonic-chip contract; acq. Luminar Semiconductor
